transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Tackle all three problems. TSP: improve baseline 405 tour using nearest-neighbor + 2-opt reasoning. Steiner: find cheaper tree connecting Bay, Pine, Grove with fewer frequencies. CJS: try to improve makespan below 217 by reordering jobs. Focus most effort on steiner (34 cap) and TSP (33 cap), then CJS (33 cap).

NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Solve all three problems: TSP 2-opt improvement, Steiner tree optimization, CJS schedule improvement", "p_solve": 0.7, "time_budget_s": 580}

I'll work through all three problems systematically.

**TSP Analysis:**
Baseline tour: [0, 8, 1, 5, 9, 11, 12, 16, 4, 3, 19, 2, 10, 13, 18, 17, 7, 15, 14, 6] = 405.046

Let me try to find a better tour using 2-opt reasoning. Key clusters:
- Right side: 1(95,14), 5(100,30), 8(75,23), 0(70,26), 9(95,50), 11(79,60), 6(85,75), 12(71,48)
- Left side: 2(17,34), 3(18,41), 4(31,37), 10(13,20), 14(26,53), 19(15,40)
- Bottom: 7(51,6), 13(35,16), 15(31,3), 17(46,10), 18(41,10)

Good tour attempt: Start at 0(70,26) → 8(75,23) → 1(95,14) → 5(100,30) → 9(95,50) → 11(79,60) → 6(85,75) → 12(71,48) → 16(51,39) → 14(26,53) → 19(15,40) → 3(18,41) → 2(17,34) → 10(13,20) → 4(31,37) → 13(35,16) → 15(31,3) → 18(41,10) → 17(46,10) → 7(51,6) → back to 0

Computing distances for this tour:
0→8: √(25+9)=5.83, 8→1: √(400+81)=21.93, 1→5: √(25+256)=16.76, 5→9: √(25+400)=20.62, 9→11: √(256+100)=18.87, 11→6: √(36+225)=16.16, 6→12: √(196+729)=30.41, 12→16: √(400+81)=21.93... wait let me recalculate: 12(71,48)→16(51,39): √(400+81)=21.93, 16→14: √(625+196)=28.65, 14→19: √(121+169)=17.03, 19→3: √(9+1)=3.16, 3→2: √(1+49)=7.07, 2→10: √(16+196)=14.56, 10→4: √(324+289)=24.76, 4→13: √(16+441)=21.38, 13→15: √(16+169)=13.60, 15→18: √(100+49)=12.21, 18→17: √(25+0)=5, 17→7: √(25+16)=6.40, 7→0: √(361+400)=27.59

Total ≈ 5.83+21.93+16.76+20.62+18.87+16.16+30.41+21.93+28.65+17.03+3.16+7.07+14.56+24.76+21.38+13.60+12.21+5+6.40+27.59 ≈ 333.92

That's much better than 405! Let me try another variant: swap 4 and 13 order, or try 10→13→15→18→17→7→4→...

Alternative: ...10→13→4→3→19→2... no that breaks flow.

Try: 0→8→1→5→9→11→6→12→16→14→19→3→2→10→4→13→15→18→17→7→0
10→4: √(324+289)=24.76, 4→13: √(16+441)=21.38 vs 10→13: √(484+16)=22.36, 13→4: same 21.38... similar.

Try: ...2→10→13→15→18→17→7→4→3→19→14→16→12... no, that's worse.

Better: 0→8→1→5→9→11→6→12→16→14→19→3→2→10→13→15→18→17→7→4→0
7→4: √(400+961)=36.9, 4→0: √(1521+169)=41.1 - bad.

Stick with: [0, 8, 1, 5, 9, 11, 6, 12, 16, 14, 19, 3, 2, 10, 4, 13, 15, 18, 17, 7]

**Steiner Analysis:**
Baseline: Bay-Port(17)+Bay-Cove(11)+Grove-Port(7)+Cove-Pine(12) = 47 edge cost, 3 frequencies = 92 total.

Try: Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Port(7)+Mill-Pine(5)+Grove-Mill(12)
That's 6 edges for 5 nodes... need tree. Terminals: Bay, Pine, Grove.

Option: Bay-Cape(2), Dune-Grove(11), Bay-Dune via Cape-Dune(13)? Bay-Cape-Dune-Grove + Pine via Grove-Pine(11) or Mill-Pine(5)+Grove-Mill(12)
Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Mill(12)+Mill-Pine(5) = 43 edge cost
Active: Bay, Cape, Dune, Grove, Mill, Pine
Interference pairs active: Bay/Cape (both active!) → need different frequencies
Bay/Cove - Cove not active, ok
Cape/Cove - Cove not active, ok
Grove/Port - Port not active, ok

Frequencies needed: Bay, Cape (must differ), Grove, Mill, Pine, Dune
Interference pairs among active: Bay/Cape → 2 colors needed for them
Any others? Check: Cliff/Ridge - not active. Cove/Port - not active. Cove/Grove - not active. Grove/Port - not active.
So only Bay≠Cape constraint. Need 2 frequencies minimum.
Total: 43 + 15*2 = 73. That's better than 92!

Let me verify tree structure: Bay-Cape, Cape-Dune, Dune-Grove, Grove-Mill, Mill-Pine - yes, 5 edges, 6 nodes, connected tree. All terminals (Bay, Pine, Grove) included. ✓

Can we do better? Try Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 37 edge cost
Active: Bay, Cape, Dune, Grove, Pine - 5 nodes
Interference: Bay/Cape active → must differ. Others: none among active.
2 frequencies needed. Total: 37 + 30 = 67. Even better!

Can we do 1 frequency? Only if no interference pairs active. Bay/Cape both active → no.

Try without Cape: Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 36 edge cost, 4 nodes
Active: Bay, Dune, Grove, Pine
Interference pairs among active: none! (Bay/Cliff-no, Bay/Cape-Cape not active, Bay/Cove-no, Grove/Port-no, Cove/Grove-no)
1 frequency! Total: 36 + 15 = 51. 

Can we do even better? Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Mill-Pine(5)+Grove-Mill(12) = 42, 5 nodes, 1 freq = 57. Worse.

Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)+Dune-Grove(11)+Dune-... wait, Bay-Dune(14) vs Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)=15. Bay-Dune direct is cheaper.

What about Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 36, 1 freq = 51.

Try: Bay-Cape(2)+Cape-Dune(13)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 37, but Bay/Cape conflict → 2 freq = 67. Worse than 51.

What about Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Mill(12)+Mill-Pine(5) = 42, 4 nodes+Mill=5 nodes
Active: Bay, Dune, Grove, Mill, Pine - interference? Bay/Cape-no, Bay/Cove-no, Grove/Port-no, Cove/Grove-no. None! 1 freq = 57. Worse than 51.

Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 36 + 15 = 51 seems best so far.

Can we find cheaper edges? Bay to Grove directly? Not listed. Bay-Port(17)+Grove-Port(7)+Grove-Pine(11)=35, active: Bay,Port,Grove,Pine. Interference: Grove/Port both active → must differ! 2 freq = 35+30=65. Worse.

Bay-Cove(11)+Cove-Pine(12)+Dune-Grove(11)+Bay-Dune(14)? That's 4 edges, 5 nodes: Bay,Cove,Pine,Dune,Grove. But need tree - is Bay connected to Grove? Bay-Dune-Grove yes. Bay-Cove-Pine. Connected. Interference: Bay/Cove both active → differ. Cove/Grove - Cove active, Grove active → differ! So Cove≠Bay and Cove≠Grove. Bay and Grove can be same. Need: Bay=1, Cove=2, Grove=1, Pine=?, Dune=?. Pine connected to Cove only, no interference with Pine listed... Pine=1 or 2 ok. Dune connected to Bay and Grove, no interference listed for Dune. 2 frequencies. Cost: 11+12+11+14=48+30=78. Worse.

So best Steiner: Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11) = 36 edge cost, 1 frequency = 51.

**CJS Analysis:**
The baseline is 217. This is complex to improve manually. I'll keep the baseline for CJS.

SUB_1: TSP: Found tour [0,8,1,5,9,11,6,12,16,14,19,3,2,10,4,13,15,18,17,7] ≈ 334, much better than 405. Steiner: Found Bay-Dune(14)+Dune-Grove(11)+Grove-Pine(11)=36 edge cost, no interference pairs among active nodes (Bay,Dune,Grove,Pine), so 1 frequency needed. Total=36+15=51, vs baseline 92. CJS: keeping baseline 217 as manual improvement is very complex.
